_doc: Comprehensive type database for Enma LSP. Rebuild with: cargo build --release

methods.doc: Extract substring from start index with given length
Index of first match, -1 if absent
Index of last match, -1 if absent
Concatenate N copies of the string

free_functions.doc: Print to console without newline. Non-string args auto-convert via string.convert() table.
Print to console with trailing newline. Supports concat: println("x = " + x)
Print a message to the attached debug console (stdout).

Variadic formatter. Accepts BOTH brace placeholders ({d}/{f}/{s}/{b}/{x}/{c}) and printf-style (%d/%f/%s/%b/%x/%c). Use whichever feels natural — they're interchangeable and can mix in the same format string. %% produces a literal %.
Convert int/uint/char (any integral) to string. For float use cast<string>(x) or format().
Convert char to single-character string. Unlike to_string('A')→"65", this returns "A".
Build a string from a char-code array
Encode int64 as hex string. Also overloaded on string → hex-encoded bytes.
Decode hex string to byte-string. hex_decode("616263") → "abc"
Parse hex string to int64. hex_to_int("ff") → 255
URL-decode a string (+ also decoded to space)

string.doc: UTF-8 string. Heap-allocated null-terminated buffer. Supports + (concat), == / < / > / <= / >=, and for-each char iteration.

methods.doc: Index of first match, -1 if missing

wstring.doc: UTF-16 wide string. Heap-allocated null-terminated uint16_t* buffer. Length counted in code units (matches Win32 wcslen).

methods.doc: Read element at index (bounds-checked, throws if OOB)
Remove element at index, returns it
Sub-array from start to end index
Index of first match, -1 if not found
Join elements into string with separator
New array with duplicates removed (preserves first occurrence)
Split into array of arrays of size n
One-level flatten (array of arrays → flat array)
Transform each element: T → T. Callback: int64 fn(int64 x)
Filter elements by predicate. Callback: int64 fn(int64 x) returns 0/1
Reduce with accumulator. Callback: int64 fn(int64 acc, int64 x)
True if any element matches predicate
True if all elements match predicate
Index of first predicate match, -1 if none
Print elements interpreted as int64 values
Print elements as int64 values with newline
Print elements interpreted as float64 values
Print elements as float64 values with newline
Print elements interpreted as string pointers
Print elements as string pointers with newline

free_functions.doc: Create array with custom stride in bytes (sign = signedness). e.g. stride=-4 for int32[], stride=+4 for uint32[].

array.doc: Growable typed array `T[]`. Elements stored packed at natural width (1/2/4/8 bytes). Out-of-bounds access throws catchable runtime exception. Destructor walks elements before freeing — string[], list<T>[], etc. drop inner allocations automatically.

methods.doc: Insert or overwrite entry for key
Read value by key (returns 0 if missing)
Read value or return fallback default
True if key exists in the map
True if any entry's value equals v
Delete entry by key, true if removed
Copy all entries from other map into this one
True if key exists. Alias of contains.
Number of entries. Alias of size.

map.doc: String-keyed hash map `map<string, V>`. Supports subscript access m["key"] and for-each iteration (for string k, V v : m).

imap.doc: Integer-keyed hash map. Same methods as map but K is int64.

methods.doc: Insert or overwrite entry for int64 key
Read value by int64 key (returns 0 if missing)
Read value or return fallback default
Alias for contains. True if key exists.
Alias for size. Number of entries.
Append to end. Alias: push. O(1).
Remove and return last. Alias: pop. 0 if empty.
Remove and return first. O(n). 0 if empty.
Insert at index (clamps OOB to end)
Remove and return element at idx. 0 if OOB.
Bounds-checked read at index. Alias: at. 0 if OOB.
Bounds-checked write at index. No-op if OOB.
True if any element equals x. Alias: has.
True if any element equals x. Alias of contains.
Append every element of other list to this one
Element at index i (0-based). Alias of get.

list.doc: Double-ended container `list<T>`. O(1) push_back/pop_back and random access. O(n) push_front/pop_front. Supports subscript lst[idx] and for-each iteration.

methods.doc: True if element exists in set
Remove element. True if it was present.
Copy contents into new array (order not guaranteed)
Add every element of other set to this one
Keep only elements present in both sets
Remove every element of other set from this one
True if all elements of this exist in other
True if both contain exactly the same elements
True if element exists. Alias of contains.

hash_set.doc: Hash set `hash_set<T>`. T must fit in 64 bits and compare by raw bits. string elements work (hashed by content, not pointer). Supports set operations: union, intersect, diff, subset check.

methods.doc: Insert or overwrite entry. O(log n).
Read value by key (returns 0 if missing)
True if key exists. O(log n).
True if key was present and removed
True if key exists. Alias of contains.

sorted_map.doc: Ordered map `sorted_map<K, V>`. O(log n) operations. Keys iterate in sorted order. K and V must fit in 64 bits and order/equate by raw bits.

methods.doc: Entire string matches the regex pattern
First match text, or empty string if none
Replace all regex matches with replacement string

regex.doc: ECMAScript regex engine. Construct with `regex re("pattern");`. Bad patterns produce null handle (0).

methods.doc: Generic type-id check against any registered type
Extract as string (only for string variants)
Raw type_id of the held value
Bypass accessors for custom boxed types
Set variant to hold an int64 value
Set variant to hold a float64 value
Set variant to hold a bool value
Set variant to hold a string (frees old string if any)

free_functions.doc: Box any registered type (dtor drops inner via reflection)

variant.doc: Open tagged union holding a value of any registered type along with its type id.

methods.doc: False if input JSON was malformed
Extract as bool (false if not bool type)
Extract as float64 (0.0 if not number)
Extract as int64 (truncated from stored double)
Extract as string (empty if not string type)

Returns string[] of object keys (insertion order)

json_value.doc: JSON value node. Returned by `json_parse()`, `json_object()`, `json_array()`. Supports tree navigation, mutation, and serialization.

file_t.doc: File stream handle. Created by file_open(), closed automatically at scope exit.

methods.doc: False if file open failed or already closed
True if at end of file
Read from current position to end of file
Write string to file, returns bytes written

mutex.doc: Backed by std::shared_mutex. Supports exclusive (writer) and shared (reader) locking.

methods.doc: Acquire exclusive (writer) lock. Blocks until available.
Non-blocking exclusive lock attempt. True if acquired.
Acquire shared (reader) lock. Concurrent shared holders allowed.
Non-blocking shared lock attempt. True if acquired.

lock_guard.doc: RAII wrapper: constructor locks mutex, destructor unlocks. Copying raises runtime error.

methods.doc: Wait on condition variable (mutex must be held). Released during wait, reacquired before return.
Resume coroutine. Returns 1 if yielded, 0 if finished.
Last yielded value from the coroutine
True if the coroutine has finished execution

coroutine_t.doc: Coroutine handle. Supports `for (T v : coroutine)` iteration. Use `yield value;` inside coroutine functions.

atomic_int32.doc: Script-exposed atomic int32. Heap-allocated, scope-dropped. Operations use std::memory_order_seq_cst.

methods.doc: CAS: swaps if current == exp. True on success.
Atomic bitwise AND; returns old value
Atomic bitwise OR; returns old value
Atomic bitwise XOR; returns old value

atomic_int64.doc: Script-exposed atomic int64. Same API as atomic_int32.

methods.doc: CAS: swaps if current == exp. True on success.
Atomic bitwise AND; returns old value
Atomic bitwise OR; returns old value
Atomic bitwise XOR; returns old value

http_response_t.doc: Result of an HTTP request. Returned by `http_get()` and `http_post()`.

methods.doc: HTTP status code (0 on transport failure / permission denied)
True if websocket connection is open
Block until message or close is received
Close connection. Use standard WS close codes (1000 = normal).
True if a valid message was received
True if peer/local close has fired
Bind to local address. Use "0.0.0.0" and port 0 for OS-picked port.
Blocking receive with timeout. Empty array on timeout/error. Max 65535 bytes.
IP address of most recent successful recv()
Port of most recent successful recv()

sound_t.doc: Loaded sound resource handle. Returned by `load_sound()`.

methods.doc: Start playback. volume 0..1, pan -1..1. Returns instance handle.

sound_inst_t.doc: Live sound playback instance. Returned by `sound.play()`.

key_state_t.doc: Atomic snapshot of keyboard key state. Returned by `get_key_state()`.

methods.doc: Fired but suppressed when modifiers are held

window_info_t.doc: Window information snapshot. Elements returned by `get_all_hwnds()`.

methods.doc: EXE basename of the owning process

proc_t.doc: Process handle for memory reading/writing. Created by `ref_process()`.

True if address is in valid range

vad_region_t.doc: Virtual memory region descriptor. Returned by `proc.virtual_query()`.

module_info_t.doc: Loaded module descriptor. Elements returned by `proc.get_module_list()`.

cpu_t.doc: Unicorn CPU emulator handle. Created by `cpu_create()` or `cpu_create_process()`. RAII — destroyed at scope exit.

Read bytes from emulated memory. Empty on failure.

zydis_req_t.doc: Zydis encoder/decoder instruction request. Used with `zydis_encode()`, `zydis_decode_to_request()`.

zydis_builder_t.doc: Zydis instruction sequence builder. Builds byte arrays from multiple instructions.

keyword_docs.new: Heap allocation operator. `new T(args)` creates a single heap object. `new T[N]` creates a heap array with N default-constructed elements. `new T[N](args)` creates N elements with the given constructor args. Returns `T*`.
keyword_docs.delete: Heap deallocation operator. `delete ptr` destroys the pointed-to object (calls its destructor) and frees the heap memory. The pointer becomes dangling after deletion.
keyword_docs.delete[]: Heap array deallocation operator. `delete[] ptr` calls the destructor on each element of a heap array, then frees the buffer. Use for arrays created with `new T[N]`. Double `delete[]` on the same pointer traps at runtime.
keyword_docs.if: Conditional branch. `if (cond) { ... } else if (cond2) { ... } else { ... }`. Supports C++17 if-init: `if (T x = expr; cond) { ... }`.
keyword_docs.for: Loop. `for (init; cond; inc) { ... }`. Also supports range-based for-each: `for (T v : container) { ... }` and key-value: `for (K k, V v : map) { ... }`.
keyword_docs.while: Pre-tested loop. `while (cond) { ... }`. Condition checked before each iteration.
keyword_docs.do: Post-tested loop. `do { ... } while (cond);`. Body runs at least once.
keyword_docs.switch: Multi-way branch. `switch (expr) { case val: ... break; default: ... }`. No implicit fallthrough — each case must end with `break`.
keyword_docs.case: Switch case label. `case value:`. Values must be compile-time constants.
keyword_docs.default: Default switch case. Matches when no other case matches.
keyword_docs.break: Exit the innermost loop or switch statement immediately.
keyword_docs.continue: Skip the rest of the current loop iteration and continue with the next one.
keyword_docs.return: Return a value from a function. `return expr;` or `return;` for void functions.
keyword_docs.try: Exception handling block. `try { ... } catch (T e) { ... }`. Destructors and `defer` blocks run during stack unwinding.
keyword_docs.catch: Exception handler. `catch (T e) { ... }` catches exceptions of type T thrown in the associated try block.
keyword_docs.throw: Throw an exception. `throw value;` or `throw StructType(args);`. Value is copied into a thread-local exception buffer.
keyword_docs.defer: Deferred execution. `defer { ... };` runs the block at scope exit, including during exception unwinding. Go-style, not in C++.
keyword_docs.goto: Unconditional jump to a label. `goto label;` then `label:`. Use sparingly.
keyword_docs.match: Pattern-match expression returning a value. `match (expr) { val1 => result1, _ => default }`. `case` keyword optional, `_` is the default arm.
keyword_docs.yield: Suspend a coroutine and return a value. `yield expr;`. Resumed via `coroutine.next()`.
keyword_docs.coroutine: Coroutine function marker. `coroutine T name(params) { ... }`. Functions suspend with `yield` and resume via the returned `coroutine_t` handle.
keyword_docs.class: Reference type with inheritance and virtual dispatch. `class Name : Base { ... };`. Methods are virtual by default when inherited.
keyword_docs.struct: Value type with C++ semantics. `struct Name { fields; methods; };`. Pass by value copies, use `T*` for reference semantics.
keyword_docs.interface: Method-signature contract. `interface Name { methods; }`. Implementers must provide all methods.
keyword_docs.mixin: Add methods to an existing struct from outside. `mixin ReturnType Struct::method(params) { ... }`.
keyword_docs.enum: Strongly-typed enumeration. `enum Name { Val1, Val2 = 5 }`. Values accessed as `Name::Val1`. Cross-enum assignment rejected.
keyword_docs.namespace: Named scope for declarations. `namespace name { ... }`. Access with `name::item`. Reopenable and nestable.
keyword_docs.using: Type alias (`using ID = int32;`) or namespace import (`using namespace ns;`).
keyword_docs.template: Generic type or function. `template<typename T> struct Box { T v; };`. Monomorphized at instantiation.
keyword_docs.typename: Template type parameter introducer. Used as `template<typename T>`.
keyword_docs.const: Runtime-initialized constant. Not reassignable after initialization.
keyword_docs.constexpr: Compile-time evaluated constant or function. Values folded to immediates in IR.
keyword_docs.static: Module-scoped global (globals are already module-scoped, so `static` is accepted but a no-op at script level).
keyword_docs.extern: Host-to-script boundary declaration. `extern ReturnType name(params);` resolved by SDK-registered native.
keyword_docs.inline: Hint or annotation to inline a function. `[[inline]]` annotation form for functions.
keyword_docs.virtual: Accepted no-op — Enma method dispatch is already vtable-based for inherited classes.
keyword_docs.override: Required marker when overriding an inherited virtual method. Compile error if no base method matches.
keyword_docs.final: Accepted no-op after class name — Enma has no sealed class concept.
keyword_docs.abstract: Placeholder keyword — Enma doesn't have a dedicated abstract class concept.
keyword_docs.shared: Placeholder keyword — not used by the current compiler.
keyword_docs.public: Accepted in struct/class bodies but fields are always accessible (no access control in scripts).
keyword_docs.private: Accepted in struct/class bodies but fields are always accessible (no access control in scripts).
keyword_docs.protected: Accepted for C++ compatibility. No access control enforced in scripts.
keyword_docs.typedef: C-style type alias. `typedef TargetType AliasName;`. Prefer `using Alias = Type;`.
keyword_docs.delegate: Typed function reference. `delegate RetType Name(params);` then assign matching functions.
keyword_docs.property: Getter/setter accessor. `property Type name { get { ... } set { ... } }`. Called without parens.
keyword_docs.operator: Operator overload. `ReturnType operator+(T other) { ... }`. Supports +, -, *, /, %, ==, <, >, <=, >=, <=>, [], []=, (), unary -, !, ~, *, ++, --.
keyword_docs.function: Placeholder keyword — functions are declared without a `function` prefix.
keyword_docs.nullable: Type modifier allowing null. `nullable T` is a distinct type that can hold null. Plain `T` cannot.
keyword_docs.out: Write-only reference parameter. `out T name` signals the param is write-only; caller's initial value is not read.
keyword_docs.volatile: Placeholder keyword — not used by the current compiler.
keyword_docs.move: Ownership transfer. `move(x)` returns x's value and nullifies the source. Subsequent access on the source traps.
keyword_docs.sizeof: Compile-time size operator. `sizeof(T)` returns byte size. Usable in `constexpr` contexts.
keyword_docs.typeof: Placeholder — use `decltype(expr)` instead for type inference.
keyword_docs.decltype: Type inference from expression at compile time. `decltype(expr) var = value;`. Works in var-decl, param types, return types.
keyword_docs.cast: Type cast. `cast<T>(x)` converts between types. Also accepts `(T)x` and `static_cast<T>(x)` forms.
keyword_docs.static_cast: C++-style type cast. `static_cast<T>(x)`. Same effect as `cast<T>(x)`.
keyword_docs.reinterpret_cast: Bit reinterpretation. `reinterpret_cast<T>(x)` reinterprets the bit pattern without conversion. Source and target must be same byte size.
keyword_docs.const_cast: Placeholder — Enma doesn't enforce constness at script level.
keyword_docs.auto: Type inference from initializer. `auto x = expr;` deduces type from the right-hand side.
keyword_docs.null: Null literal. Assignable to any pointer type or `nullable T`. Also spelled `nullptr`.
keyword_docs.nullptr: Null pointer literal. Identical to `null`. Assignable to any pointer type.
keyword_docs.this: Pointer to the current object inside methods. Use `this->field` or access fields directly.
keyword_docs.void: Empty type representing no value. Used for function return types that return nothing.
keyword_docs.bool: Boolean type. 1 byte. Values: `true` or `false`. Truthy check on int/float/pointer: non-zero = true.
keyword_docs.char: 8-bit character. 1 byte. Signed. Can hold ASCII and UTF-8 code units.
keyword_docs.wchar: Wide character. 2 bytes. UTF-16 code unit. Used with `wstring` for Win32 compatibility.
keyword_docs.int8: Signed 8-bit integer. Range: -128 to 127. Stored in 1 byte, widened to 64-bit for arithmetic.
keyword_docs.int16: Signed 16-bit integer. Range: -32768 to 32767. Stored in 2 bytes.
keyword_docs.int32: Signed 32-bit integer. Range: approx -2.1B to 2.1B. Stored in 4 bytes.
keyword_docs.int64: Signed 64-bit integer. Native arithmetic width. All integer math evaluates at 64-bit internally.
keyword_docs.uint8: Unsigned 8-bit integer. Range: 0 to 255. Stored in 1 byte.
keyword_docs.uint16: Unsigned 16-bit integer. Range: 0 to 65535. Stored in 2 bytes.
keyword_docs.uint32: Unsigned 32-bit integer. Range: 0 to approx 4.3B. Stored in 4 bytes.
keyword_docs.uint64: Unsigned 64-bit integer. Range: 0 to approx 1.8e19. Stored in 8 bytes.
keyword_docs.aint8: Atomic 8-bit integer. 1 byte. Built-in atomic primitive (LOCK-prefixed ops).
keyword_docs.aint16: Atomic 16-bit integer. 2 bytes. Built-in atomic primitive.
keyword_docs.aint32: Atomic 32-bit integer. 4 bytes. Built-in atomic primitive.
keyword_docs.aint64: Atomic 64-bit integer. 8 bytes. Built-in atomic primitive.
keyword_docs.float32: IEEE 754 single-precision float. 4 bytes. Narrowed at native ABI but maintains 64-bit view script-side. Literals: `1.5f`.
keyword_docs.float64: IEEE 754 double-precision float. 8 bytes. Default float type. `float` and `double` are aliases.
keyword_docs.string: UTF-8 heap-allocated string. Supports + (concat), ==/!=/< (lexicographic compare), subscript (read-only char codes), for-each char iteration.
keyword_docs.wstring: UTF-16 wide string. Heap-allocated null-terminated `uint16_t*` buffer. Length in code units (matches Win32 `wcslen`). Surrogate pairs count as 2.
